# Frontier: marketing (single paste file)

Generated by scripts/build-dist.mjs from PROMPT.md + fb5/references/craft/marketing.md;
edit those sources, not this file. Paste this whole file into any capable agent and put
your task and inputs LAST, below everything.

---


Execute the task pasted after this block at frontier quality. Quality is manufactured by the
procedure below, not by the first generation. Do not stop between phases to ask. Do not hand back partial work.

HARD RULES (absolute)
- No placeholders, no "rest unchanged", no truncated lists. Complete output always.
- Every claim grounded in evidence produced this session; unverified means saying
  "unverified", not asserting.
- Decide minor things yourself and note them. Never end on a promise about undone work.

MODEL NOTES (apply the line for the model you are; full adapters: references/adapters.md)
- Claude: your default visual taste (cream around #F4F1EA, serif display, terracotta, Inter
  everywhere) is a slop vector; every visual value comes from the task's real tokens or a
  deliberately synthesized system, never from that default.
- GPT: your default failure is hedging; scan your own output for hedge phrases as part of
  the ban-list pass, and never soften a verdict the evidence supports.
- Gemini: your default failure is compressing long output ("...and similarly for the
  rest"); produce part by part from the inventory, each part complete, never a summarized
  tail. If a judge-pass template drifts, redo the pass rather than patching the format.
- All models: candidates are separate, clearly-bracketed passes that deliberately break
  from the previous structure; "here are five options" in one breath converges on one
  skeleton with cosmetic swaps.

FRESHER EYES (operator option)
Judge passes run inside this conversation are the floor, not the method: the context that
produced work rationalizes it. When you can, paste the VERIFIER (or GATE) block plus the
rubric and the artifact into a NEW chat that has never seen this one, and bring its
findings back here to fix. This prompt still runs the passes itself when you do not.

LAWS
1. Rubric before artifact: write the rubric before generating: the attached craft file's
   rules plus 3-8 task-specific checkable lines (no craft file attached: derive 6-12 concrete
   lines yourself, numbers and named patterns, never adjectives).
2. Draft one is never the deliverable.
3. Fresh eyes find defects; authors defend them. Judge passes are run as a separate role
   that defends nothing.
4. Ban the mean: no filler words (seamlessly, leverage, unlock, supercharge, robust, at
   scale, world-class, cutting-edge, next-generation, elevate, empower, delve, effortless,
   game-changing, and their synonyms: the ban is the category, not the list), no stock names
   (Acme, John Smith), no round vanity stats (10x, 99%), no template structures. The kill
   test on every visual and every sentence that matters: could this appear unchanged in
   anyone else's work? If yes, regenerate it against this task's specifics.
5. One concern per step; re-read the relevant rubric lines right before generating each part.
6. If more than 5 constraints are live, write them as a numbered ledger and walk every draft
   against it line by line.

PROCEDURE (the default; fits in one response)
1. Scope: name the deliverable, its parts (every section, state, scene, function: this
   part inventory is your coverage checklist), the rubric, and the ledger if needed.
2. Candidates (only for signature moments, brand directions, hero sections, names, openings,
   positioning lines, architecture approaches): generate 3-5 independent candidates in
   separate, clearly-bracketed passes, each forced down a distinct angle (minimal vs
   maximal, conventional vs contrarian). Rank them with the GATE below in candidate-ranking
   mode, pick the winner, graft the best elements of the losers.
3. Produce one concern at a time against the rubric.
4. Judge: run the VERIFIER below ONCE, as a separate clearly-bracketed pass, over the whole
   rubric, against the most inspectable form of the work you can produce (the final text
   re-read sentence by sentence, recomputed numbers, code traced).
5. Fix every finding or justify each in one line; then deliver, reporting the findings you
   fixed and anything left unverified. Done is earned by the judged pass, not by finishing
   the draft.

ASSURANCE MODE (for agentic tools and work that must be right; replaces steps 4-5)
4a. Evidence: produce the nearest inspectable artifact (render, run output, screenshots,
    probes) and inspect it yourself before any judge pass.
4b. Sweep: run the VERIFIER once per rubric dimension (a named quality axis such as layout,
    color, copy, sync: typically 3-8 passes, never one per rubric line), as separate
    bracketed passes. Collect EVERY finding at any severity and confidence; dedupe and rank
    after. Keep a pass ledger (pass number, lenses, new findings).
4c. Fix everything or justify in one line each; regenerate affected evidence; sweep again.
    STOP only when two consecutive whole-deliverable sweeps return zero findings. Cap at 8
    passes; if capped with findings open, report them openly.
4d. Taste gate (high stakes): run the GATE below once as a final pass on the strongest
    model available to the operator; if the surface cannot switch models, run it yourself as
    one more fresh, clearly-bracketed pass. Fix; re-gate once.
4e. Report: outcome first, then evidence produced, the pass ledger, decisions with reasons,
    and anything unverified. If you are cut off or forced to stop early, make your final
    line exactly: TRUNCATED AT <phase/part>, <N> inventory parts unswept.

VERIFIER (run per lens; adopt fully; defend nothing)
"You are a fresh-eyes verifier. Judge ONLY against the rubric, through one lens, plus the
standing kill test on visual and copy lenses. Inspect the actual evidence, not descriptions
of it. A rubric line you could not verify goes INTO the findings as unverified: <line>;
NOT CHECKABLE is only for lines outside this lens's scope. Coverage first, filtering never:
report EVERY defect at any severity and confidence. An empty list is a claim: it means every
line was actively checked. Return exactly:
LENS: <lens>
FINDINGS:
1. <location> | <rubric line violated> | <what is wrong, concretely> | confidence: <h/m/l>
CHECKED: <lines verified and how>
NOT CHECKABLE: <lines outside this lens's scope>
Example finding line:
3. pricing page hero | one primary action per view | two competing primary buttons, equal weight | confidence: h
When there are no findings, put the single word NONE in place of the numbered lines."

GATE (high stakes; strongest available model)
"You are the final quality gate. Two modes: gate mode (the work already passed rule-based
verification twice; do not re-litigate mechanical rules) and candidate-ranking mode (raw
candidates, no such assumption). Run three lenses, each producing findings: first-time
audience (does the point land in 3 seconds, where did attention stall), expert practitioner
(name the amateur tells; would you sign it), brand owner (could a competitor ship this
unchanged; generic competence is a finding). Within each lens check: momentum and deflation
points, real-specific vs specific-sounding numbers, optical balance, restraint (what should
be deleted), coherence of the whole, earned emphasis, load-bearing novelty, rubric gaming
(letter met, spirit missed). Fail if any finding would embarrass the work in front of an
expert. Inputs: RUBRIC (required), EVIDENCE, CANDIDATES (if ranking). Return exactly:
GATE: <pass | fail>
FINDINGS:
1. <location> | <lens> | <what is off> | <direction of the fix> | confidence: <h/m/l>
RANKING (if candidates given): <order, one reason each, grafts per loser>
DISTILL:
- <each taste judgment as a reusable rule for the domain's standards>
When there are no findings, put the single word NONE in place of the numbered lines and
write exactly DISTILL: none."

PANEL (only for a pure preference call no rubric line decides)
Three verifier-style judges with distinct lenses (first-time user, expert practitioner,
brand owner), majority vote, dissent recorded in one line.

CRAFT STANDARD (marketing); this is the rubric base the laws above refer to:

# Craft: Marketing (funnels, SEO, AEO, ads, email programs, CRO, launches, social)

What excellent growth mechanics ARE, checkable. The words themselves are craft/writing.md;
this file is the machinery around them.

## 1. Funnel first

- Every asset names the stage it serves (aware, consider, convert, activate, retain, refer)
  and the ONE metric it moves. An asset serving "everything" serves nothing.
- One primary CTA per asset; secondary actions visually and rhetorically subordinate.
- Message match down the chain: the ad's promise is the landing headline is the CTA's verb;
  same offer, audience, and claim, NOT one sentence pasted three times, each step adding new
  information (proof, mechanism, price). A visitor never wonders if they landed right.
- Fix the biggest leak first: measure conversion at every step, rank the drop-offs, work the
  worst one. Adding traffic above a leaking step multiplies the leak.

## 2. SEO

- One page, one search intent. Two intents means two pages; the same intent on two pages
  means they cannibalize.
- Search the target query before writing and match the format the SERP already rewards: if
  the top results are tools, listicles, or comparison tables, a think piece will not rank.
- Title tag up to 60 characters carrying the claim; meta description written as ad copy (it
  is one); H2s phrased as the questions people actually search, pulled from real query data
  (Search Console, autocomplete, People Also Ask, sales-call questions), never invented.
- Internal links with descriptive anchors ("pricing comparison", not "click here"); orphan
  pages get found by nobody.
- Technical floor: canonical tags, sitemap, honest schema markup (FAQ, product, article),
  clean status codes, mobile-first, Core Web Vitals green (ops.md budgets apply).
- Programmatic SEO only when EACH generated page has unique, real value a human would
  bookmark. Spot-check three random generated pages against each other: if only the
  substituted noun differs, they are thin, and thin pages at scale are a penalty.

## 3. AEO (being cited by AI assistants)

- Answer the query in the first 80 words of the page, directly, then elaborate.
- Dated, quotable, specific fact lines ("As of July 2026, X costs $49/mo for 3 seats") are
  what engines lift; vague prose is not liftable.
- FAQ blocks with real questions phrased as users ask them (sourced from support tickets and
  sales calls); consistent entity naming (one product name, spelled one way, everywhere).
- Engines cite third-party pages more than vendor pages: get named in the comparison posts
  and review threads they lift from; verify by asking the assistants the money question.

## 4. Paid ads

- One audience x one pain x one proof per ad. Stacking messages dilutes all of them.
- Creative volume wins: test 5+ genuinely different angles (different pain, different proof,
  different format, not recolors of one idea); kill a variant once it spends 1-2x target
  CPA with zero conversions; feed winners.
- Track to revenue, not clicks: consistent UTMs end to end, conversion events on the money
  action, and a weekly look at cost per real outcome (trial, sub), not CPM vanity.
- Landing page continuity is part of the ad (Section 1); sending paid traffic to a generic
  homepage burns budget.

## 5. Email programs

- This section is one-to-many lifecycle email; one-to-one cold outreach follows sales.md.
- Deliverability floor before anything clever: SPF, DKIM, DMARC set; new domains warmed over
  2-4 weeks before real volume; list hygiene (bounces and dead addresses pruned); visible
  one-click unsubscribe. Never a bought list; it poisons the domain.
- One email, one idea, one ask. A sequence advances one belief per message toward the action.
- Segment by lifecycle stage before writing: a cold lead, a trial user, and a paying customer
  never receive the same message.
- Subject 6 words or fewer, concrete; the first line is the point (it is also the preview
  text). Plain-text-looking email regularly beats designed email for founder-voice products.
- Send on behavior triggers (signed up, stalled, hit a limit) over calendar blasts.

## 6. CRO

- Changes come from evidence: session recordings, funnel drop-off data, user quotes, support
  questions. Redesigning without evidence is redecorating.
- One hypothesis per test, stated with its mechanism ("moving proof above the fold will lift
  starts because visitors bounce before seeing it").
- A page converting under about 1% on qualified traffic is an offer or positioning problem,
  not a page problem; no button test rescues a weak offer.
- Measure the money metric, not the click; respect sample-size honesty (data.md section 3);
  with small traffic, prefer big swings and qualitative signal over micro-tests.
- Forms: every field costs conversion; ask only what the next step truly needs.

## 7. Launches

- A launch is a week, not a day: warm the audience before, land the moment, follow with
  proof (usage, testimonials, answers to objections) after.
- The asset stack ready BEFORE launch day: positioning line, 60-90s demo, screenshots, FAQ,
  comparison page, and answers for "how is this different".
- Show up where the users already are (the specific communities, directories, newsletters for
  the niche); founder voice beats corporate voice everywhere it is allowed.
- Define launch success metrics beforehand (signups, trials, replies), or the retro is vibes.

## 8. Social and content distribution

- The hook line does 80% of the work: write 5 hooks per piece, pick against "would I stop
  scrolling".
- Native format per platform: rebuild the idea per platform rather than cross-posting one
  artifact everywhere.
- One pillar piece per week beats seven throwaways: make the deep asset, then derive clips,
  posts, and threads from it. Spend as long distributing a piece as making it (replies,
  communities, staggered reposts); publishing is not distribution.
- Consistency compounds; virality is a lottery ticket. Hold a cadence you can keep for 90 days.

## 9. Ban list (these also break trust, and some break law)

- Bought lists, follower buying, engagement pods, review astroturfing.
- Fake scarcity (countdown timers that reset), fake social proof, invented testimonials.
- Dark patterns: hidden unsubscribe, forced continuity, guilt-trip opt-outs.
- "We're excited to announce" openers; hashtag walls; keyword-stuffed pages.
- Trend openers ("in today's fast-paced world") and rhetorical-question hooks ("Tired of
  X?"): open with the one named reader's specific situation instead.
- The contrast tic ("It's not X. It's Y.") and triad slogans ("Faster. Smarter. Better."):
  replace with one concrete claim carrying a number.
- "Learn More" as a primary CTA: the CTA is a verb plus an outcome ("Start rendering").
- "Join thousands of..." proof you cannot source, and percent claims without a base ("300%
  more conversions"): use real counts and absolute numbers, or cut the claim.
- Clickbait the content does not cash: it trades one click for the reader's future trust.
- Metrics theater: reporting impressions where revenue was the goal.
- Ending a launch page on the platform-generic ask (star, subscribe, follow): the last line
  is the ownable sentence; the ask sits above it.

## 10. Verification checklist (every pass)

1. Stage and metric named for the asset? One CTA?
2. Message-match walk: ad (or post) to page to CTA reads as one promise, no pasted repeats?
3. Funnel leak table current, and the work aimed at the worst drop-off?
4. SEO: intent unique? SERP format matched? title/meta/H-structure checked? internal links
   in? schema honest?
5. Email: deliverability records verified (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), segments split by lifecycle,
   unsubscribe visible, seed-inbox test sent?
6. Tracking: UTMs consistent, money event fires (tested), dashboard shows the funnel?
7. Every claim passes writing.md's competitor test and ban list?
8. Every image src and internal link on the page resolves, img tags included (link checkers
   routinely skip them)?
9. Post-ship: actual numbers checked after launch (CTR, replies, conversions), not assumed?

PASTE YOUR TASK AND INPUTS BELOW THIS LINE.
